title: ClusterRole for Cross-Namespace Access
description:
Grant a ServiceAccount read access to ConfigMaps across ALL namespaces using a ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding.

Create `configmap-reader` ClusterRole (get/list), `cm-reader-sa` ServiceAccount, and a ClusterRoleBinding. Verify the SA can list configmaps in `kube-system` but cannot create them.

**Common Pitfalls**: ClusterRoleBinding is cluster-wide — use only when cross-namespace access is needed. ConfigMaps are in `apiGroups: [""]`. The SA `namespace` field is still required in the subjects block.
steps: Create a ServiceAccount named cm-reader-sa in the default namespace
Create a ClusterRole named configmap-reader with rules: apiGroups [""], resources ["configmaps"], verbs ["get", "list"]
Create a ClusterRoleBinding named configmap-reader-binding that binds configmap-reader to cm-reader-sa
Verify SA can list configmaps in kube-system: kubectl auth can-i list configmaps --as system:serviceaccount:default:cm-reader-sa -n kube-system
Verify SA cannot create configmaps: kubectl auth can-i create configmaps --as system:serviceaccount:default:cm-reader-sa (should output 'no')
hints: ClusterRole and ClusterRoleBinding have no namespace in their metadata
In ClusterRoleBinding subjects, still specify namespace: default for the ServiceAccount
kubectl auth can-i list configmaps -n kube-system tests namespace-scoped access; the SA should answer 'yes' due to the ClusterRoleBinding
ConfigMaps belong to apiGroups: [""] — the empty string core API group

editor_config.initial_code:
# ServiceAccount
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: cm-reader-sa
  namespace: default
---
# ClusterRole: read configmaps cluster-wide
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: configmap-reader
rules:
- apiGroups: # TODO: [""] for core API
  resources: # TODO: ["configmaps"]
  verbs: # TODO: ["get", "list"]
---
# ClusterRoleBinding
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: configmap-reader-binding
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: cm-reader-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: configmap-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: cm-reader-sa
  namespace: default
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRole
metadata:
  name: configmap-reader
rules:
- apiGroups: [""]
  resources: ["configmaps"]
  verbs: ["get", "list"]
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: configmap-reader-binding
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: cm-reader-sa
  namespace: default
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: configmap-reader
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml
echo "✓ Resources applied"

# ServiceAccount
kubectl get serviceaccount cm-reader-sa -n default > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ ServiceAccount cm-reader-sa exists" || { echo "✗ cm-reader-sa not found"; exit 1; }

# ClusterRole
kubectl get clusterrole configmap-reader > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ ClusterRole configmap-reader exists" || { echo "✗ ClusterRole configmap-reader not found"; exit 1; }

# Verify ClusterRole rules
resources=$(kubectl get clusterrole configmap-reader -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].resources[*]}')
if echo "$resources" | grep -q "configmaps"; then
  echo "✓ ClusterRole targets configmaps"
else
  echo "✗ ClusterRole resources do not include configmaps. Found: $resources"
  exit 1
fi

verbs=$(kubectl get clusterrole configmap-reader -o jsonpath='{.rules[0].verbs[*]}')
if echo "$verbs" | grep -q "list" && echo "$verbs" | grep -q "get"; then
  echo "✓ ClusterRole includes get and list verbs"
else
  echo "✗ ClusterRole verbs incomplete. Found: $verbs"
  exit 1
fi

# ClusterRoleBinding
kubectl get clusterrolebinding configmap-reader-binding > /dev/null 2>&1 && echo "✓ ClusterRoleBinding configmap-reader-binding exists" || { echo "✗ ClusterRoleBinding not found"; exit 1; }

# Test can list configmaps in kube-system
can_list=$(kubectl auth can-i list configmaps --as system:serviceaccount:default:cm-reader-sa -n kube-system)
if [ "$can_list" = "yes" ]; then
  echo "✓ cm-reader-sa CAN list configmaps in kube-system"
else
  echo "✗ cm-reader-sa cannot list configmaps in kube-system (got: $can_list)"
  exit 1
fi

# Test cannot create configmaps
can_create=$(kubectl auth can-i create configmaps --as system:serviceaccount:default:cm-reader-sa -n default)
if [ "$can_create" = "no" ]; then
  echo "✓ cm-reader-sa CANNOT create configmaps (correct)"
else
  echo "✗ cm-reader-sa should NOT be able to create configmaps (got: $can_create)"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
